response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. This means she uses 1/2 ounce per day. In a leap year, there are 366 days. To find out how much shampoo she needs, we calculate 366 days × 1/2 ounce/day = 183 ounces. She buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles. To find out how many bottles she needs, we divide 183 ounces by 14 ounces per bottle. This gives us 13.071 bottles. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs 14 bottles. [number of bottles] [total ounces needed]  

Brittany needs 14 bottles of shampoo to get through a leap year. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so 14 bottles provide 196 ounces of shampoo. This is enough for 366 days, as she uses 183 ounces. The calculation is accurate and follows the rules of the problem. [total ounces needed] [number of bottles]
